title: Canonicalize the fresh-slick option-richness block and prune aliases
summary: Keep the public-backed fresh-slick feasibility feature family, but remove redundant aliases and duplicate flags to reduce split noise and CV-only overfitting.
rationale: The fresh-slick feasibility family appears useful, but many variants likely produce nearly identical predictions through duplicate boolean flags, repeated thresholds, or alias features. Treating those aliases as independent evidence can inflate confidence without adding real signal.
implementation_hint: Replace the fresh-slick option-richness section with a compact canonical version containing only option count, softest viable slick, minimum or best finish margin, different-slick feasibility, NeedsStop_And_DifferentSlickCanFinish, and three per-compound finish margins for soft, medium, and hard. Add ablation switches for the full block, compact block, and simpler finish-window baseline.
expected_effect: Small to medium potential ROC AUC improvement, mainly through greater validation stability and fewer random holdout gains. Confidence is medium to high because the underlying feature family has practical evidence.
risk: Over-pruning may remove a useful signal variant. Leakage risk is low if all features use only current lap, compound, tyre life, and remaining-lap estimates.
